title: arthropodium hispidum
intro: Arthropodium hispidum, also known as the cat's tongue or chocolate lily, is a type of flowering plant native to New Zealand and Australia. It is a perennial herb with long, thin leaves that resemble a cat's tongue, and produces small, white or purple flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which have been found to have potential health benefits in the treatment of various ailments, including wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The roots of the plant can be prepared as a decoction or tincture for medicinal use, and it is also used in herbal preparations such as ointments and creams for topical application.
